BIB                 12         62                                 C3057001    2 
TITLE      Double-differential primary target neutron yields      C3057001    3 
           from dual-thick-target proton and heavy ion            C3057001    4 
           accelerator experiments                                C3057001    5 
AUTHOR     (H.N.Ratliff,N.A.McGirl,M.R.Beach,L.A.Castellanos,     C3057001    6 
           M.S.Clowdsley,L.H.Heilbronn,Ch.Latessa,J.W.Norbury,    C3057001    7 
           A.Rusek,M.Sivertz,A.P.Srikrishna,H.-Ch.Wang,C.Zeitlin) C3057001    8 
REFERENCE  (J,NIM/B,542,87,2023)                                  C3057001    9 
           #doi:10.1016/j.nimb.2023.06.001                        C3057001   10 
INSTITUTE  (1USATEN,2JPNJAE,1USALAN,1USABNL)                      C3057001   11 
           (2NORNOR) Western Norway University of Applied         C3057001   12 
           Sciences, Inndalsveien 28, 5063 Bergen, Norway         C3057001   13 
           (2ITYITY) University of Trento, Via Calepina,          C3057001   14 
           14-38122 Trento TN, Italy                              C3057001   15 
           (1USAUSA) Leidos, Inc., 2625 Bay Area Blvd., Houston,  C3057001   16 
           TX 77058, USA                                          C3057001   17 
FACILITY   (SYNCH,1USABNL) The NASA Space Radiation Laboratory    C3057001   18 
            (NSRL), located on the campus of Brookhaven National  C3057001   19 
           Laboratory.                                            C3057001   20 
DETECTOR   (SCIN) Six cylindrical 5" x 5"  (12.7 cm diameter by   C3057001   21 
           12.7 cm length) organic liquid scintillators (OLS)     C3057001   22 
           placed at differing angles with respect to the beamlineC3057001   23 
            and center of the primary target.                     C3057001   24 
METHOD     (TOF) Double differential thick-target yields of       C3057001   25 
           neutrons and light ions were determined from fifteen   C3057001   26 
           GCR-like source beams incident upon eight different    C3057001   27 
           shielding configurations. Unique to this experiment    C3057001   28 
           was the dual-target system employed wherein a          C3057001   29 
           secondary target was placed several meters downstream  C3057001   30 
           of the primary target, allowing penetrating source     C3057001   31 
           ions and the fragments from interactions in the        C3057001   32 
           primary target to then travel downstream and interact  C3057001   33 
           in this secondary target. This dual-target             C3057001   34 
           configuration was designed to mimic enclosed           C3057001   35 
           environments where additional reactions in the second  C3057001   36 
           wall are a significant source of radiation risk,       C3057001   37 
           particularly from neutrons. The kinetic energies of    C3057001   38 
           all particles produced in the upstream target were     C3057001   39 
           determined with time-of-flight techniques.             C3057001   40 
ANALYSIS   The offline analysis was broken into two broad         C3057001   41 
           categories: (1) separate good neutron events from all  C3057001   42 
           other events and (2) characterize neutron energies     C3057001   43 
           from times of flight and quantify uncertainties.       C3057001   44 
ERR-ANALYS (ERR-S) Statistical uncertainties.                     C3057001   45 
           (ERR-SYS) Total systematic uncertainties.              C3057001   46 
           (ERR-1) Solid angle: in the solid angle normalization  C3057001   47 
           since neutrons from the primary target can be          C3057001   48 
           produced anywhere along its length.                    C3057001   49 
           (ERR-2) Efficiency: uncertainty is assumed in the      C3057001   50 
           efficiency correction performed using SCINFUL-QMD      C3057001   51 
           generated efficiency curves.                           C3057001   52 
           (ERR-3) Graphical: uncertainty attributable to the     C3057001   53 
           human judgment involved in the data analysis process,  C3057001   54 
           such as the delineation of gamma-ray events from       C3057001   55 
           neutron events in two-dimensional pulse shape plots.   C3057001   56 
COMMENT    Negative values are due to background subtraction      C3057001   57 
           procedure. Due to large volume of data, only primary   C3057001   58 
           target neutrons are given in this compilation. Data    C3057001   59 
           for primary and secondary targets are available at     C3057001   60 
           the following Mendeley repository:                     C3057001   61 
           https://data.mendeley.com/datasets/myjky5f6f5/1        C3057001   62 
STATUS     (APRVD) Approved by H.N.Ratliff on May 26, 2025.       C3057001   63
